findings.summary: generateInvoice spans 204 lines — past the 60-line threshold for a single function. Bodies this size usually mix unrelated responsibilities, and an agent editing one section often misses interactions in another.

suggested_actions.description: Extract cohesive sections into named helpers so each responsibility can be read, tested, and edited in isolation.

findings.summary: Repo ships no agent-readable instruction files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md). Agents loading this repo will not see project commands, conventions, or safety rules and are more likely to make confident-but-wrong edits.
evidence: no AGENTS.md found at repo root
no CLAUDE.md found at repo root
package.json declares bin(s): messy — agents have no way to discover commands

suggested_actions.description: Add AGENTS.md or a Claude skill so coding agents can discover repo conventions before editing.

findings.summary: 7 direct uses of Date.now()/new Date(). Reading the system clock in domain code makes behaviour non-deterministic and couples tests to wall time.
evidence: each call observes wall time at runtime — tests cannot pin a fixed moment without monkey-patching

suggested_actions.description: Inject a clock/now() abstraction so domain code is deterministic in tests and free of hidden temporal coupling.

findings.summary: 5 direct uses of Date.now()/new Date(). Reading the system clock in domain code makes behaviour non-deterministic and couples tests to wall time.
evidence: each call observes wall time at runtime — tests cannot pin a fixed moment without monkey-patching

suggested_actions.description: Inject a clock/now() abstraction so domain code is deterministic in tests and free of hidden temporal coupling.

findings.summary: 14 TODO/FIXME markers (424.2 per 1k LOC). Each marker is intended behaviour the author flagged as not-final — too many in one file makes it hard for an agent to tell which logic is load-bearing.

suggested_actions.description: Convert each marker into a tracked issue or remove it. Lingering TODOs mislead humans and agents about which behaviour is intentional.
